title: Hyperbolic (NumPy)
description: Explicit finite-volume march (HyperbolicSolver). Runs locally in the browser via Pyodide — no server needed. IMEX / splitting schemes live in zoomy_core.fvm.solver_imex_numpy / solver_splitting_numpy.
template:
import os
from zoomy_core.fvm.solver_numpy import HyperbolicSolver
import zoomy_core.fvm.timestepping as ts
from zoomy_core.misc.misc import Zstruct

# HDF5-first: the solver writes the fields and we write the mesh to the
# same file. The visualisation layer reads that file back through
# zoomy_plotting.read_hdf5 — no in-memory threading, no auto-save
# sniffing, and lazy per-slice reads come for free.
_h5_path = '/tmp/zoomy_sim/sim.h5'
os.makedirs(os.path.dirname(_h5_path), exist_ok=True)

# Release the previous run's h5py handle before truncating the same path.
close_store()

settings = Zstruct(output=Zstruct(
    directory=os.path.dirname(_h5_path),
    filename=os.path.splitext(os.path.basename(_h5_path))[0],
    snapshots=20,
    clean_directory=True,
))

from zoomy_core.numerics import NumericalSystemModel, ReconstructionSpec
# reconstruction order/limiter live on the NSM now (not the solver ctor);
# from_system_model also coerces a raw model / SystemModel correctly.
nsm = NumericalSystemModel.from_system_model(
    model, reconstruction=ReconstructionSpec(order=1))

solver = HyperbolicSolver(
    time_end=0.1,
    compute_dt=ts.adaptive(CFL=0.3),
    settings=settings,
)

# Write the mesh in 'w' mode first, solver appends /fields/ in 'a' mode.
mesh.write_to_hdf5(_h5_path)
solver.solve(mesh, nsm, write_output=True)

# open_hdf5 installs a zoomy_plotting.SimulationStore as scope['store'].
open_hdf5(_h5_path)

title: IMEX (NumPy)
description: IMEX march — explicit Riemann flux + implicit (Newton/GMRES) source (IMEXSolver). Runs locally in the browser via Pyodide — no server needed.
template:
import os
from zoomy_core.fvm.solver_imex_numpy import IMEXSolver
import zoomy_core.fvm.timestepping as ts
from zoomy_core.misc.misc import Zstruct

# IMEX: explicit Riemann flux + implicit (Newton/GMRES) source. Same
# HDF5-first pattern as the Hyperbolic card — mesh written first, the
# solver appends /fields/, and open_hdf5 hands the store to the viz layer.
_h5_path = '/tmp/zoomy_sim/sim.h5'
os.makedirs(os.path.dirname(_h5_path), exist_ok=True)

close_store()

settings = Zstruct(output=Zstruct(
    directory=os.path.dirname(_h5_path),
    filename=os.path.splitext(os.path.basename(_h5_path))[0],
    snapshots=20,
    clean_directory=True,
))

from zoomy_core.numerics import NumericalSystemModel, ReconstructionSpec
nsm = NumericalSystemModel.from_system_model(
    model, reconstruction=ReconstructionSpec(order=1))

solver = IMEXSolver(
    time_end=0.1,
    compute_dt=ts.adaptive(CFL=0.3),
    settings=settings,
)

mesh.write_to_hdf5(_h5_path)
solver.solve(mesh, nsm, write_output=True)

open_hdf5(_h5_path)

title: Hyperbolic (JAX)
description: Explicit finite-volume march (HyperbolicSolver) on the JAX/GPU backend — needs the zoomy-jax container.

title: IMEX (JAX)
description: IMEX march — explicit flux + implicit source (IMEXSourceSolverJax) on the JAX/GPU backend — needs the zoomy-jax container.

title: Hyperbolic (AMReX)
description: Explicit finite-volume march on block-structured AMR (HyperbolicSolver) — needs the zoomy-amrex container.

title: Split (AMReX)
description: Chorin pressure-projection split (SplitSolver; needs a chorin_split model) on AMReX — needs the zoomy-amrex container. Pressure preconditioner is fixed at 3 downstream (the precond knob is currently dead).

title: Hyperbolic (DMPlex)
description: Explicit finite-volume march on a PETSc DMPlex unstructured mesh — needs the zoomy-dmplex container.
